be danger from weapons (i.e. war). The following is the instruction
of Sage Garga for remedying the evil effects of this portent.
[The compound —-can also be interpreted as—"Unnatural
changes seen in house-hold appurtenances such as ladles,
winnowing baskets etc" Our commentator takes the words
separately.]

When these aerial portents are witnessed, the king ought to
worship the Wind-God with the flour of barley, and devout and
pure Brahmanas should chant repeatedly the five hymns beginning

388 Brhat SamhitH
with liA Vuyoh". The king should also please Brahmanas with milk
porridge and monetary gifts. Similarly, offerings should he made
into the sacred fire with devotion and efforts, along with large-scale
feeding of the worthy and generous gifts of gold.
[We are not expected to sit in judgment over the language •
and expressions of ancient Sages like Garga. A moderh editor has
pointed out that the word is against Panini's rule.
